perturbation.original_text: You will not copy or attempt to make copies (written, photocopied, or otherwise) of any Exam Material, including, without limitation, any exam questions or answers;
perturbation.changed_text: You will not copy or attempt to make copies (written, or otherwise) of any Exam Material, including, without limitation, any exam questions or answers; however, the proctor is authorized to create a digital back up of the exam material.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by initially forbidding any copies of Exam Materials, then creating an exception for the proctor to create a digital backup. The original text forbides any copies of exam material, without limitations. With the changed text, the text forbides all copies, but allows for digital back ups.
perturbation.contradicted_text: You will not copy or attempt to make copies (written, or otherwise) of any Exam Material, including, without limitation, any exam questions or answers; however, the proctor is authorized to create a digital back up of the exam material.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that 'You will not copy or attempt to make copies... of any Exam Material, including, without limitation, any exam questions or answers;'. The changed text includes the caveat 'however, the proctor is authorized to create a digital back up of the exam material.'. This creates an in-text contradiction as the initial statement forbids all copies, without limitation, while the added clause allows for a specific type of copy (digital backup by the proctor). The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, which means the contradiction exists within the same section, between two clauses of the same section. This aligns with the definition of Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction: '...when different sections of a contract provide conflicting deadlines, obligations, or penalties. This creates in-text contradiction regarding which terms should be enforced, leading to disputes over contractual obligations.'
